title: Cylindrolobus brachystachyus
taxonomy: Cylindrolobus brachystachyus is classified as a species within the genus Cylindrolobus, which is part of the family Melastomataceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome or memecyclid family. The Melastomataceae family is part of the order Myrtales, which is a larger group of plants that also includes the families of the willow, tea, and myrtle. The order Myrtales is further classified under the subclass Rosidae, which is a large group of flowering plants. The subclass Rosidae is part of the class Magnoliopsida, also known as the flowering plants.
uses: Cylindrolobus brachystachyus is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and stems are said to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses.
